misses.query: I've got my first court hearing coming up - will the judge just make a final decision then or is it more like a starting point?
What actually happens at the FDR hearing - does the judge make a decision or just give their opinion?
The social worker wrote a report about my child's health for the adoption case - will I get to see it or is it kept secret?
What does cross-examination mean - do I have to question my ex myself at the final hearing?
My child has been taken by their other parent and a court order has been made against me - it says something about a Tipstaff, what does that mean and do I have to do what they say?
I've got a final hearing coming up for my child arrangements case and I have no idea what to expect - what do people like me usually get wrong?
What happens if I miss the deadline the court set for sending in my documents?
My case is already going through the courts but I need to ask for something extra - like a change to an existing order. What form do I need to fill in?
My ex is hiding in a women's refuge and I need to get court papers to them - how does that actually work without me finding out where they're staying?
My ex has loads of money and is burying me in paperwork and lengthy letters, is there anything the court can do to stop them using their money against me like this?
I've been told I need to apply online for a child arrangements order - can I do that through the online system, or do I have to print it out and take it to court?
How long do I have to serve the application on the other person after it's been issued by the court?
I signed an agreement with my ex about splitting our finances but we didn't go through the court - is that enough to make it official?
The judge kept cutting me off at the first hearing and said they didn't need to hear everything I wanted to say — can they do that?
My child has been taken abroad without my permission - what kind of court order can force the other parent to come to court and bring them back?
The judge gave me a list of things I need to do before the next hearing – do I actually have to do all of them or are some optional?
My ex took our kids abroad and won't bring them back - can the English courts still deal with our case even though the children aren't here right now?
What does FDR mean? The court has listed something called an FDR hearing and I don't know what it is

results.query: I stayed home to look after the kids for 15 years while my wife worked, does that mean I get less of the house when we divorce?

I gave up my career to raise our kids while my wife built up her pension - does that count for nothing now we're divorcing?

I've been told I need to bring a questionnaire and a chronology to my FDA hearing - what are these and when do I need to hand them in?

The social worker wrote a report about my child's health for the adoption case - will I get to see it or is it kept secret?
Do I have to get a solicitor to fill in Form A or can I do it myself?
What does cross-examination mean - do I have to question my ex myself at the final hearing?
We're still married even after the conditional order comes through – is that right?
The other side has legal aid but I've been told I might get my costs paid - what does it mean when it says the court has to decide if it's 'reasonable' before I can get the money?
My child was adopted but I'm trying to get a declaration of parentage - will the court use their adoptive name or their birth name on the paperwork?
What is a children's guardian and will the court automatically appoint one for my child in court proceedings?
My child has been taken by their other parent and a court order has been made against me - it says something about a Tipstaff, what does that mean and do I have to do what they say?

What happens if I miss the deadline the court set for sending in my documents?
Do I need to send my position statement to the other side before the hearing, or just give it to the court?
I got divorced two years ago and we never sorted out the finances - is it too late to do anything about it now?
The judge at my FDR said what they think should happen - does that mean it's decided? Do I have to accept it?
My case is already going through the courts but I need to ask for something extra - like a change to an existing order. What form do I need to fill in?
My ex has just told me they got a big bonus after we both sent in our financial forms. Do they have to tell the court about that or can they just keep quiet about it?

Do I have to go to mediation before I can apply to court, or can I just go straight to the judge?
Can the court order a DNA test to find out if someone is the father of my child?

If I send in Form A will my ex find out straight away?

Can I do my appeal in the High Court online instead of going in person?
How long do I have to serve the application on the other person after it's been issued by the court?

I can't afford a solicitor - is there any way to get help paying for a non-molestation order?
What form do I need to fill in to apply for a temporary order during my divorce proceedings?

I signed an agreement with my ex about splitting our finances but we didn't go through the court - is that enough to make it official?

Will my divorce hearing be open to the public or held behind closed doors?
Can I get divorced first and sort out the money stuff later, or is that a bad idea?

I want to appeal the judge's decision but I'm confused about all the different types of judges mentioned in the paperwork - what do terms like 'circuit judge level' and 'district judge level' actually mean?
How do I address the judge at my final hearing? I don't want to get it wrong and look stupid.
